title: 10 medicinal constituents of Buddleja officinalis (butterfly bush)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Buddleja officinalis ursolic acid is a triterpenoid compound that is isolated from the leaves of the plant. It has been reported to possess various bioactive properties, including anti-inflammatory, antitumor, and antimicrobial activities. Ursolic acid has also been shown to exhibit antioxidant effects and may play a role in protecting against chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. Further research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and applications.

Buddleja officinalis oleanolic acid is a type of triterpenoid compound extracted from its leaves and flowers. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. Studies have shown that oleanolic acid exhibits potent antioxidant activity, which can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, making it a potential therapeutic agent for various diseases.

Buddleja officinalis quercetin is a type of flavonoid compound that has been isolated from its leaves and flowers. This bioactive compound has been found to possess numerous biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer properties. Quercetin has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory disorders, lowering cholesterol levels, and reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease.

Buddleja officinalis kaempferol is a type of flavonoid compound found in the leaves and flowers of this popular ornamental plant. Kaempferol has been extensively studied for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. It has been shown to have efficacy in protecting against oxidative stress and reducing inflammation in various diseases, such as cardiovascular disease, cancer, and neurodegenerative disorders.

Buddleja officinalis luteolin is a flavonoid compound that has been identified in the flowers and leaves of this plant. It exhibits various biological activities, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Luteolin has been shown to have potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, making it a subject of ongoing research for its therapeutic applications.

Buddleja officinalis gallic acid is a phenolic compound found in its leaves and stems. This acid has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, including antibacterial and antioxidant activities. It has also shown promise in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, making it a promising candidate for the treatment of various diseases.

Buddleja officinalis epigallocatechin gallate is a polyphenolic compound extracted from its leaves. It has been studied for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anticancer properties. Studies have shown that EGCG can inhibit the growth of cancer cells and induce apoptosis (cell death) in certain types of tumors. Its potent antioxidant activity also makes it a promising agent in the prevention of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and neurodegenerative disorders.

Buddleja officinalis chlorogenic acid is a natural compound found in its leaves and stems. This bioactive substance has been studied for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. Chlorogenic acid has been shown to exhibit inhibitory effects on the growth of certain cancer cells and may help reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. Its chemical structure is a combination of quinic acid and caffeic acid, which provides its unique biological activities.

Buddleja officinalis caffeic acid is a polyphenolic compound found in its flowers and leaves. This compound has been extensively studied for its potential medicinal properties, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. It has been shown to exhibit significant protective effects against oxidative stress and cellular damage, making it a promising natural product for the development of new therapeutic agents.

Buddleja officinalis sinapic acid is a type of phenolic acid that has been isolated from its flowers. This compound has been found to possess various biological activities, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. It has also been reported to exhibit potential therapeutic effects in the treatment of diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disorders.

intro: Buddleja officinalis has active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes. These bioactive compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. For instance, the flavonoids may help alleviate respiratory problems by reducing inflammation in the airways, while the phenolic acids could contribute to the plant's antiseptic properties, aiding in wound healing and infection prevention. These constituents have the potential to improve people's lives by providing relief from chronic conditions and promoting overall well-being.
intro_desc: Buddleja officinalis has active constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. These constituents confer anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, which can alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and respiratory issues. The flavonoids, for instance, can improve cardiovascular health by reducing blood pressure and lipid levels, while the alkaloids can ease pain and inflammation, thereby improving quality of life for individuals with chronic conditions.
